tools.name: aliases.assignAlias
tools.description: Creates a new alias for the deployment with the given deployment ID. The authenticated user must own this deployment. If the desired alias is already assigned to another deployment, then it will be removed from the old deployment and assigned to the new one.

tools.name: aliases.deleteAlias
tools.description: Delete an Alias with the specified ID.

tools.name: aliases.getAlias
tools.description: Retrieves an Alias for the given host name or alias ID.

tools.name: aliases.listAliases
tools.description: Retrieves a list of aliases for the authenticated User or Team. When `domain` is provided, only aliases for that domain will be returned. When `projectId` is provided, it will only return the given project aliases.

tools.name: aliases.listDeploymentAliases
tools.description: Retrieves all Aliases for the Deployment with the given ID. The authenticated User must own the deployment.

tools.name: artifacts.artifactExists
tools.description: Check that a cache artifact with the given `hash` exists. This request returns response headers only and is equivalent to a `GET` request to this endpoint where the response contains no body.

tools.name: artifacts.artifactQuery
tools.description: Query information about an array of artifacts.

tools.name: artifacts.downloadArtifact
tools.description: Downloads a cache artifact indentified by its `hash` specified on the request path. The artifact is downloaded as an octet-stream. The client should verify the content-length header and response body.

tools.name: artifacts.recordEvents
tools.description: Records an artifacts cache usage event. The body of this request is an array of cache usage events. The supported event types are `HIT` and `MISS`. The source is either `LOCAL` the cache event was on the users filesystem cache or `REMOTE` if the cache event is for a remote cache. When the event is a `HIT` the request also accepts a number `duration` which is the time taken to generate the artifact in the cache.

tools.name: artifacts.status
tools.description: Check the status of Remote Caching for this principal. Returns a JSON-encoded status indicating if Remote Caching is enabled, disabled, or disabled due to usage limits.

tools.name: artifacts.uploadArtifact
tools.description: Uploads a cache artifact identified by the `hash` specified on the path. The cache artifact can then be downloaded with the provided `hash`.

tools.name: authentication.createAuthToken
tools.description: Creates and returns a new authentication token for the currently authenticated User. The `bearerToken` property is only provided once, in the response body, so be sure to save it on the client for use with API requests.

tools.name: authentication.deleteAuthToken
tools.description: Invalidate an authentication token, such that it will no longer be valid for future HTTP requests.

tools.name: authentication.emailLogin
tools.description: Request a new login for a user to get a token. This will respond with a verification token and send an email to confirm the request. Once confirmed you can use the verification token to get an authentication token.

tools.name: authentication.getAuthToken
tools.description: Retrieve metadata about an authentication token belonging to the currently authenticated User.

tools.name: authentication.listAuthTokens
tools.description: Retrieve a list of the current User's authentication tokens.

tools.name: authentication.verifyToken
tools.description: Verify the user accepted the login request and get a authentication token. The user email address and the token received after requesting the login must be added to the URL as a query string with the names `email` and `token`.

tools.name: certs.getCertById
tools.description: Get cert by id

tools.name: certs.issueCert
tools.description: Issue a new cert

tools.name: certs.removeCert
tools.description: Remove cert

tools.name: certs.uploadCert
tools.description: Upload a cert

tools.name: checks.createCheck
tools.description: Creates a new check. This endpoint must be called with an OAuth2 or it will produce a 400 error.

tools.name: checks.getAllChecks
tools.description: List all of the checks created for a deployment.

tools.name: checks.getCheck
tools.description: Return a detailed response for a single check.

tools.name: checks.rerequestCheck
tools.description: Rerequest a selected check that has failed.

tools.name: checks.updateCheck
tools.description: Update an existing check. This endpoint must be called with an OAuth2 or it will produce a 400 error.

tools.name: deployments.cancelDeployment
tools.description: This endpoint allows you to cancel a deployment which is currently building, by supplying its `id` in the URL.

tools.name: deployments.createDeployment
tools.description: Create a new deployment with all the required and intended data. If the deployment is not a git deployment, all files must be provided with the request, either referenced or inlined. Additionally, a deployment id can be specified to redeploy a previous deployment.

tools.name: deployments.deleteDeployment
tools.description: This API allows you to delete a deployment, either by supplying its `id` in the URL or the `url` of the deployment as a query parameter. You can obtain the ID, for example, by listing all deployments.

tools.name: deployments.getDeployment
tools.description: Retrieves information for a deployment either by supplying its ID (`id` property) or Hostname (`url` property). Additional details will be included when the authenticated user is an owner of the deployment.

tools.name: deployments.getDeploymentEvents
tools.description: Get the build logs of a deployment by deployment ID and build ID. It can work as an infinite stream of logs or as a JSON endpoint depending on the input parameters.

tools.name: deployments.getDeploymentFileContents
tools.description: Allows to retrieve the content of a file by supplying the file identifier and the deployment unique identifier. The response body will contain the raw content of the file.

tools.name: deployments.getDeployments
tools.description: List deployments under the account corresponding to the API token. If a deployment hasn't finished uploading (is incomplete), the `url` property will have a value of `null`.

tools.name: deployments.listDeploymentBuilds
tools.description: Retrieves the list of builds given their deployment's unique identifier.

tools.name: deployments.listDeploymentFiles
tools.description: Allows to retrieve the file structure of a deployment by supplying the deployment unique identifier.

tools.name: deployments.uploadFile
tools.description: Before you create a deployment you need to upload the required files for that deployment. To do it, you need to first upload each file to this endpoint. Once that's completed, you can create a new deployment with the uploaded files. The file content must be placed inside the body of the request. In the case of a successful response you'll receive a status code 200 with an empty body.

tools.name: dns.createRecord
tools.description: Creates a DNS record for a domain.

tools.name: dns.getRecords
tools.description: Retrieves a list of DNS records created for a domain name. By default it returns 20 records if no limit is provided. The rest can be retrieved using the pagination options.

tools.name: dns.removeRecord
tools.description: Removes an existing DNS record from a domain name.

tools.name: dns.updateRecord
tools.description: Updates an existing DNS record for a domain name.

tools.name: domains.buyDomain
tools.description: Allows to purchase the specified domain.

tools.name: domains.checkDomainPrice
tools.description: Check the price to purchase a domain and how long a single purchase period is.

tools.name: domains.checkDomainStatus
tools.description: Check if a domain name is available for purchase.

tools.name: domains.createOrTransferDomain
tools.description: This endpoint is used for registering a new domain name with Vercel for the authenticating user, and also for initiating a domain transfer request from an external Registrar to Vercel.

tools.name: domains.deleteDomain
tools.description: Delete a previously registered domain name from Vercel. Deleting a domain will automatically remove any associated aliases.

tools.name: domains.getDomain
tools.description: Get information for a single domain in an account or team.

tools.name: domains.getDomainConfig
tools.description: Get a Domain's configuration.

tools.name: domains.getDomains
tools.description: Retrieves a list of domains registered for the authenticating user. By default it returns the last 20 domains if no limit is provided.

tools.name: edgeConfig.createEdgeConfig
tools.description: Creates an Edge Config.

tools.name: edgeConfig.createEdgeConfigToken
tools.description: Adds a token to an existing Edge Config.

tools.name: edgeConfig.deleteEdgeConfig
tools.description: Delete an Edge Config by id.

tools.name: edgeConfig.deleteEdgeConfigTokens
tools.description: Deletes one or more tokens of an existing Edge Config.

tools.name: edgeConfig.getEdgeConfig
tools.description: Returns an Edge Config.

tools.name: edgeConfig.getEdgeConfigItem
tools.description: Returns a specific Edge Config Item.

tools.name: edgeConfig.getEdgeConfigItems
tools.description: Returns all items of an Edge Config.

tools.name: edgeConfig.getEdgeConfigs
tools.description: Returns all Edge Configs.

tools.name: edgeConfig.getEdgeConfigToken
tools.description: Return meta data about an Edge Config token.

tools.name: edgeConfig.getEdgeConfigTokens
tools.description: Returns all tokens of an Edge Config.

tools.name: edgeConfig.patchtEdgeConfigItems
tools.description: Update multiple Edge Config Items in batch.

tools.name: edgeConfig.updateEdgeConfig
tools.description: Updates an Edge Config.

tools.name: integrations.deleteConfiguration
tools.description: Allows to remove the configuration with the `id` provided in the parameters. The configuration and all of its resources will be removed. This includes Webhooks, LogDrains and Project Env variables.

tools.name: integrations.getConfiguration
tools.description: Allows to retrieve a the configuration with the provided id in case it exists. The authenticated user or team must be the owner of the config in order to access it.

tools.name: integrations.getConfigurations
tools.description: Allows to retrieve all configurations for an authenticated integration. When the `project` view is used, configurations generated for the authorization flow will be filtered out of the results.

tools.name: integrations.getV1IntegrationsSearchRepo
tools.description: Lists git repositories linked to a namespace `id` for a supported provider. A specific namespace `id` can be obtained via the `git-namespaces`  endpoint. Supported providers are `github`, `gitlab` and `bitbucket`. If the provider or namespace is not provided, it will try to obtain it from the user that authenticated the request.

tools.name: integrations.gitNamespaces
tools.description: Lists git namespaces for a supported provider. Supported providers are `github`, `gitlab` and `bitbucket`. If the provider is not provided, it will try to obtain it from the user that authenticated the request.

tools.name: logDrains.createConfigurableLogDrain
tools.description: Creates a configurable log drain. This endpoint must be called with a team AccessToken (integration OAuth2 clients are not allowed)

tools.name: logDrains.createLogDrain
tools.description: Creates an Integration log drain. This endpoint must be called with an OAuth2 client (integration), since log drains are tied to integrations. If it is called with a different token type it will produce a 400 error.

tools.name: logDrains.deleteConfigurableLogDrain
tools.description: Deletes a Configurable Log Drain. This endpoint must be called with a team AccessToken (integration OAuth2 clients are not allowed). Only log drains owned by the authenticated team can be deleted.

tools.name: logDrains.deleteIntegrationLogDrain
tools.description: Deletes the Integration log drain with the provided `id`. When using an OAuth2 Token, the log drain can be deleted only if the integration owns it.

tools.name: logDrains.getConfigurableLogDrain
tools.description: Retrieves a Configurable Log Drain. This endpoint must be called with a team AccessToken (integration OAuth2 clients are not allowed). Only log drains owned by the authenticated team can be accessed.

tools.name: logDrains.getConfigurableLogDrains
tools.description: Retrieves a list of Configurable Log Drains. This endpoint must be called with a team AccessToken (integration OAuth2 clients are not allowed). Only log drains owned by the authenticated team can be accessed.

tools.name: logDrains.getIntegrationLogDrains
tools.description: Retrieves a list of all Integration log drains that are defined for the authorized account. When using an OAuth2 token, the list is limited to log drains created by the authenticated integration.

tools.name: projects.addProjectDomain
tools.description: Add a domain to the project by passing its domain name and by specifying the project by either passing the project `id` or `name` in the URL. If the domain is not yet verified to be used on this project, the request will return `verified = false`, and the domain will need to be verified according to the `verification` challenge via `POST /projects/:idOrName/domains/:domain/verify`. If the domain already exists on the project, the request will fail with a `400` status code.

tools.name: projects.createProject
tools.description: Allows to create a new project with the provided configuration. It only requires the project `name` but more configuration can be provided to override the defaults.

tools.name: projects.createProjectEnv
tools.description: Create one ore more environment variables for a project by passing its `key`, `value`, `type` and `target` and by specifying the project by either passing the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.deleteProject
tools.description: Delete a specific project by passing either the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.editProjectEnv
tools.description: Edit a specific environment variable for a given project by passing the environment variable identifier and either passing the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.filterProjectEnvs
tools.description: Retrieve the environment variables for a given project by passing either the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.getProject
tools.description: Get the information for a specific project by passing either the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.getProjectDomain
tools.description: Get project domain by project id/name and domain name.

tools.name: projects.getProjectDomains
tools.description: Retrieve the domains associated with a given project by passing either the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.getProjectEnv
tools.description: Retrieve the environment variable for a given project.

tools.name: projects.getProjects
tools.description: Allows to retrieve the list of projects of the authenticated user. The list will be paginated and the provided query parameters allow filtering the returned projects.

tools.name: projects.removeProjectDomain
tools.description: Remove a domain from a project by passing the domain name and by specifying the project by either passing the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.removeProjectEnv
tools.description: Delete a specific environment variable for a given project by passing the environment variable identifier and either passing the project `id` or `name` in the URL.

tools.name: projects.updateProject
tools.description: Update the fields of a project using either its `name` or `id`.

tools.name: projects.updateProjectDomain
tools.description: Update a project domain's configuration, including the name, git branch and redirect of the domain.

tools.name: projects.verifyProjectDomain
tools.description: Attempts to verify a project domain with `verified = false` by checking the correctness of the project domain's `verification` challenge.

tools.name: secrets.createSecret
tools.description: Allows to create a new secret.

tools.name: secrets.deleteSecret
tools.description: This deletes the user's secret defined in the URL.

tools.name: secrets.getSecret
tools.description: Retrieves the information for a specific secret by passing either the secret id or name in the URL.

tools.name: secrets.getSecrets
tools.description: Retrieves the active Vercel secrets for the authenticated user. By default it returns 20 secrets. The rest can be retrieved using the pagination options. The body will contain an entry for each secret.

tools.name: secrets.renameSecret
tools.description: Enables to edit the name of a user's secret. The name has to be unique to that user's secrets.

tools.name: teams.createTeam
tools.description: Create a new Team under your account. You need to send a POST request with the desired Team slug, and optionally the Team name.

tools.name: teams.deleteTeam
tools.description: Delete a team under your account. You need to send a `DELETE` request with the desired team `id`. An optional array of reasons for deletion may also be sent.

tools.name: teams.deleteTeamInviteCode
tools.description: Delete an active Team invite code.

tools.name: teams.getTeam
tools.description: Get information for the Team specified by the `teamId` parameter.

tools.name: teams.getTeamAccessRequest
tools.description: Check the status of a join request. It'll respond with a 404 if the request has been declined. If no `userId` path segment was provided, this endpoint will instead return the status of the authenticated user.

tools.name: teams.getTeamMembers
tools.description: Get a paginated list of team members for the provided team.

tools.name: teams.getTeams
tools.description: Get a paginated list of all the Teams the authenticated User is a member of.

tools.name: teams.inviteUserToTeam
tools.description: Invite a user to join the team specified in the URL. The authenticated user needs to be an `OWNER` in order to successfully invoke this endpoint. The user can be specified with an email or an ID. If both email and ID are provided, ID will take priority.

tools.name: teams.joinTeam
tools.description: Join a team with a provided invite code or team ID.

tools.name: teams.patchTeam
tools.description: Update the information of a Team specified by the `teamId` parameter. The request body should contain the information that will be updated on the Team.

tools.name: teams.removeTeamMember
tools.description: Remove a Team Member from the Team, or dismiss a user that requested access, or leave a team.

tools.name: teams.requestAccessToTeam
tools.description: Request access to a team as a member. An owner has to approve the request. Only 10 users can request access to a team at the same time.

tools.name: teams.updateTeamMember
tools.description: Update the membership of a Team Member on the Team specified by `teamId`, such as changing the _role_ of the member, or confirming a request to join the Team for an unconfirmed member. The authenticated user must be an `OWNER` of the Team.

tools.name: user.getAuthUser
tools.description: Retrieves information related to the currently authenticated User.

tools.name: user.listUserEvents
tools.description: Retrieves a list of "events" generated by the User on Vercel. Events are generated when the User performs a particular action, such as logging in, creating a deployment, and joining a Team (just to name a few). When the `teamId` parameter is supplied, then the events that are returned will be in relation to the Team that was specified.

tools.name: user.requestDelete
tools.description: Initiates the deletion process for the currently authenticated User, by sending a deletion confirmation email. The email contains a link that the user needs to visit in order to proceed with the deletion process.

tools.name: webhooks.createWebhook
tools.description: Creates a webhook

tools.name: webhooks.deleteWebhook
tools.description: Deletes a webhook

tools.name: webhooks.getWebhook
tools.description: Get a webhook

tools.name: webhooks.getWebhooks
tools.description: Get a list of webhooks

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
